executive_summary.key_insight: ControlPartner is a well-established Norwegian automation specialist with solid financial health and strong technical capabilities in water infrastructure and maritime sectors. Recent acquisition by Uniwater (Oct 2025) provides strategic backing but introduces integration risks. Limited scale and geographic concentration remain key constraints.

conditions: Confirm Uniwater acquisition completion and integration plan
Obtain parent company guarantee for contracts >NOK 10M
Require key person retention clauses for critical project personnel
Establish clear escalation path to Uniwater management for dispute resolution
Conduct site audit of Porsgrunn facilities before contract award

profile.description: ControlPartner AS is a Norwegian specialist in operational control and automation systems, serving municipal water/wastewater and maritime sectors. Founded in 2015 (predecessor from 1996), the company provides complete automation systems with SCADA, operational control facilities, and electrical systems integration. Acquired by Swedish water infrastructure group Uniwater in October 2025.

advantages: Deep expertise in Norwegian water infrastructure standards
Strong local service presence in Porsgrunn, Bergen, Oslo
Long-term client relationships in municipal sector
Now part of larger Uniwater group with access to capital

risks.description: Recent acquisition by Uniwater creates integration uncertainty and potential key personnel departures
risks.mitigation: Require key person retention guarantees; establish clear communication protocols with Uniwater management

risks.description: Small scale limits ability to absorb large project losses or warranty claims
risks.mitigation: Parent company guarantee from Uniwater; phased payment terms; performance bonds

risks.description: Geographic concentration in Norway limits diversification and growth options
risks.mitigation: Uniwater acquisition provides access to Swedish market; monitor expansion plans

risks.description: Dependence on municipal sector budget cycles creates revenue volatility
risks.mitigation: Diversification into maritime sector; Uniwater cross-selling opportunities

timeline.milestone: Expansion to Bergen and Oslo offices

operational_capability.key_facilities: Headquarters Porsgrunn with offices in Bergen and Oslo

negotiation_notes: Uniwater acquisition may create pricing pressure to win market share
Strong preference for long-term service contracts
Willing to negotiate on payment terms for strategic clients
Limited flexibility on hourly rates for specialist engineers
Value-based pricing approach for complex automation projects

conditions: Obtain sustainability policy and carbon footprint data
Verify compliance with Norwegian working conditions regulations
Confirm anti-corruption policies and supplier code of conduct
Request diversity and inclusion metrics for technical workforce
